questions.question_text: How many sides does a quadrilateral have?
questions.subject: mathematics
questions.answer_explanation: Quadrilateral means 'four sides' (quad = four, lateral = sides). It's a closed 2D polygon with 4 sides, 4 vertices, and 4 angles.

questions.question_text: Which of the following is NOT a type of quadrilateral?
questions.subject: mathematics
questions.answer_explanation: Pentagon has 5 sides. Quadrilaterals include: rectangle, square, parallelogram, rhombus, trapezium, and kite - all with 4 sides.

options.explanation: Rectangle is a quadrilateral with 4 right angles
Trapezium is a quadrilateral with one pair of parallel sides
Correct! Pentagon has 5 sides, not 4
Rhombus is a quadrilateral with all sides equal

questions.question_text: Which quadrilateral has diagonals that are equal and bisect each other at 90°?
questions.subject: mathematics
questions.answer_explanation: Square has diagonals that are: (1) equal in length, (2) bisect each other, and (3) perpendicular (meet at 90°). Rectangle has equal diagonals but not at 90°.

questions.question_text: What is the sum of all interior angles of a quadrilateral?
questions.subject: mathematics
questions.answer_explanation: Sum of interior angles = (n-2) × 180° where n = number of sides. For quadrilateral: (4-2) × 180° = 2 × 180° = 360°.

options.explanation: This is sum for a triangle (3 sides)
Correct! All quadrilaterals have angle sum = 360°
This is sum for a pentagon (5 sides)
This is sum for a hexagon (6 sides)

questions.question_text: In a parallelogram, one angle is 80°. What are the measures of the other three angles?
questions.subject: mathematics
questions.answer_explanation: In parallelogram: opposite angles are equal, adjacent angles are supplementary (sum=180°). If one angle = 80°, opposite = 80°. Adjacent angles = 180° - 80° = 100°.

options.explanation: Correct! Opposite angles equal (80°), adjacent supplementary (100°)
Sum would be 360° but angles don't follow parallelogram rules
Opposite angles must be equal in parallelogram
Adjacent angles sum should be 180°

questions.question_text: In a parallelogram, opposite angles are:
questions.subject: mathematics
questions.answer_explanation: Key property of parallelogram: opposite angles are equal. Also, adjacent angles are supplementary (sum = 180°).

options.explanation: Correct! Opposite angles in parallelogram are always equal
Adjacent angles are supplementary, not opposite
Complementary means sum = 90°, not true here
Opposite angles are always equal in parallelogram

questions.question_text: Is it possible to have a quadrilateral with all obtuse angles (each > 90°)?
questions.subject: mathematics
questions.answer_explanation: False - If all 4 angles are obtuse (>90°), their sum would exceed 4×90° = 360°. But quadrilateral angle sum must equal exactly 360°. Impossible!

options.explanation: Four obtuse angles would sum to more than 360°
Correct! Sum would exceed 360°, which violates angle sum property

questions.question_text: In a trapezium ABCD with AB∥CD, if ∠A = 55° and ∠B = 70°, what is ∠D?
questions.subject: mathematics
questions.answer_explanation: In trapezium, angles on same side (between parallel lines) are supplementary: ∠A + ∠D = 180°. So ∠D = 180° - 55° = 125°.

options.explanation: This is ∠C (180° - 70°), not ∠D
∠D is not equal to ∠A in trapezium
This is ∠B, not ∠D

questions.question_text: If angles of a parallelogram are in ratio 1:2, and one angle is x, what are all angles?
questions.subject: mathematics
questions.answer_explanation: Adjacent angles sum to 180°: x + 2x = 180°, so 3x = 180°, x = 60°. Angles are: 60°, 120°, 60°, 120° (opposite angles equal).

options.explanation: These don't sum to 180° for adjacent angles
This is rectangle, not matching 1:2 ratio
Adjacent angles don't sum to 180°

questions.question_text: If angles of a quadrilateral are in ratio 2:5:4:1, what is the smallest angle?
questions.subject: mathematics
questions.answer_explanation: Let angles be 2x, 5x, 4x, x. Sum: 2x + 5x + 4x + x = 360° → 12x = 360° → x = 30°. Smallest angle = x = 30°.

options.explanation: Correct! 12x = 360° → x = 30° (smallest ratio value)
This is 2x, not the smallest (x)
This is 4x, not smallest
This is 5x (largest), not smallest

questions.question_text: A quadrilateral with opposite sides equal is called:
questions.subject: mathematics
questions.answer_explanation: Parallelogram has opposite sides equal and parallel. This includes rectangles, squares, and rhombuses as special cases.

options.explanation: Trapezium has only one pair of parallel sides
Kite has two pairs of adjacent sides equal, not opposite
Correct! Parallelogram has both pairs of opposite sides equal
Cyclic means vertices lie on a circle, not about sides

questions.question_text: In a rhombus with ∠ABC = 50°, what is ∠ACD? (AC is diagonal)
questions.subject: mathematics
questions.answer_explanation: In rhombus, diagonal bisects the angle. ∠BCD = ∠ABC = 50° (opposite angles). Diagonal AC bisects ∠BCD, so ∠ACD = 50°/2 = 25°.

options.explanation: Correct! Diagonal bisects angle: ∠ACD = 50°/2 = 25°
That's the full angle ∠ABC, not half
Diagonal bisects the angle, giving 25°
Diagonals are perpendicular, but that's not ∠ACD

questions.question_text: Which property is TRUE for a rectangle?
questions.subject: mathematics
questions.answer_explanation: Rectangle has all angles = 90°, opposite sides equal, and diagonals equal (but not perpendicular). It's a special parallelogram.

options.explanation: Only square has all sides equal
Correct! Rectangle has four right angles
Rectangle diagonals are equal but not perpendicular

options.option_text: Only one pair of sides is parallel
options.explanation: Both pairs of opposite sides are parallel

questions.question_text: Which property is TRUE for a rhombus?
questions.subject: mathematics
questions.answer_explanation: Rhombus has all sides equal, opposite angles equal, diagonals bisect each other at 90°, but angles are NOT all 90° (that's a square).

options.explanation: Only square (special rhombus) has all angles 90°
Correct! Rhombus has all four sides equal
Rhombus diagonals are unequal (unlike rectangle)
All sides are equal, not just opposite

questions.question_text: A square is a special case of:
questions.subject: mathematics
questions.answer_explanation: Square satisfies properties of rectangle (all angles 90°), rhombus (all sides equal), and parallelogram (opposite sides parallel). It's the most specific quadrilateral.

options.explanation: Square is also rhombus and parallelogram
Square is also rectangle and parallelogram
Correct! Square has properties of all three
Square has two pairs of parallel sides, not one

questions.question_text: In a kite, which sides are equal?
questions.subject: mathematics
questions.answer_explanation: Kite has two pairs of consecutive (adjacent) equal sides. Like: AB = AD and CB = CD. Opposite sides are NOT equal.

options.explanation: Kite has adjacent sides equal, not opposite
Correct! Kite has two pairs of consecutive equal sides
All sides equal would be rhombus, not kite
Kite specifically has adjacent sides equal

questions.question_text: A trapezium has how many pairs of parallel sides?
questions.subject: mathematics
questions.answer_explanation: Trapezium has exactly one pair of parallel sides (called bases). The other two sides are non-parallel (called legs).

questions.question_text: Diagonals of a rectangle are:
questions.subject: mathematics
questions.answer_explanation: Rectangle diagonals are equal in length and bisect each other, but are NOT perpendicular. Only square has perpendicular diagonals among rectangles.

options.explanation: Correct! Rectangle diagonals are equal and bisect each other
Equal yes, but perpendicular only in square
Rectangle diagonals are equal, not unequal
Diagonals are equal and do bisect each other

questions.question_text: Diagonals of a rhombus:
questions.subject: mathematics
questions.answer_explanation: Rhombus diagonals bisect each other at 90° (perpendicular) and bisect the angles, but are NOT equal in length (unlike rectangle).

options.explanation: Rhombus diagonals are unequal (only square has equal)
Correct! Diagonals are perpendicular bisectors
Diagonals intersect, they're not parallel
They do bisect each other at 90°

questions.question_text: If one angle of a parallelogram is a right angle, then it is a:
questions.subject: mathematics
questions.answer_explanation: If one angle of parallelogram = 90°, all angles = 90° (opposite angles equal, adjacent supplementary). This makes it a rectangle.

options.explanation: Rhombus has all sides equal, not necessarily 90° angles
Correct! One 90° angle makes all angles 90° in parallelogram
Need all sides equal too, not just 90° angles
It's still a parallelogram, specifically rectangle

questions.question_text: Angle bisectors of a parallelogram form which quadrilateral?
questions.subject: mathematics
questions.answer_explanation: When angle bisectors of a parallelogram intersect, they form a rectangle. The angles where bisectors meet are all 90°.

questions.question_text: In a parallelogram, diagonal divides it into:
questions.subject: mathematics
questions.answer_explanation: One diagonal of a parallelogram divides it into two congruent (equal) triangles by SSS or SAS congruence. Both triangles have same area.

options.explanation: Triangles formed are congruent (equal)
Correct! Diagonal creates two equal triangles
One diagonal makes 2 triangles, not 4
Diagonal creates triangles, not rectangles

questions.question_text: Midpoints of sides of any quadrilateral, when joined, form:
questions.subject: mathematics
questions.answer_explanation: Varignon's theorem: Joining midpoints of any quadrilateral's sides forms a parallelogram. This is always true, regardless of the original quadrilateral type.

options.explanation: Only if diagonals of original are equal
Correct! Varignon's theorem - always forms parallelogram
Only if diagonals of original are perpendicular
Only if diagonals are equal and perpendicular

questions.question_text: If diagonals of a parallelogram are equal, it is a:
questions.subject: mathematics
questions.answer_explanation: Parallelogram with equal diagonals is a rectangle. If diagonals are also perpendicular, it becomes a square.

questions.question_text: If diagonals of a quadrilateral bisect each other at right angles, it is a:
questions.subject: mathematics
questions.answer_explanation: Quadrilateral with diagonals that bisect each other perpendicularly (at 90°) is a rhombus. If also equal, it's a square.
